item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1+
I loved the general Idea about
I hope you make tale about His father or his grandma
I just love.the concept behind it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Considered to fall into the Monkey's Paw Demonic Contract category, though this is only speculation.

But if "Monkey's Paw Demonic Contract" is a category made by the Foundation for use by the Foundation, shouldn't they be able to tell if this entity qualified, or test it to see if it did or not? Why would they need to speculate when they can test it? It kind of feels like the National Bird Society saying we have a bird in a cage in front of us that eats seeds and has red feathers, so we consider it to be a member of Cardinalidae but that's only speculation we don't know for sure.

Anyways, I love this! My favorite part was the Test Logs which were all delightfully detailed. I love how you start with a bunch of doctors asking for awesome personal superpowers with increasingly weird results and then you get the succinct redacted Test 11. I'm especially happy with how you treated the human test subjects in this— I feel like very often there are SCPs about an anomaly that has little regard for human life and the researchers study it with even less regard for human life. Having willing participants and not immediately killing them off/using them for tragedy is actually really refreshing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Dr. Blake was hospitalized on 29.11.2019 after an encounter with a Physeter macrocephalus on a research expedition."
After discovering this meant a sperm whale, I got up from my seat at the library, excused myself to the bathroom, and subsequently bellowed with laughter.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll edit the comment to be more subjective, because that's not at what i meant.
I thought about nitpicking the things i disliked, but I rather just say what felt like the heart of the issues I had with it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't disagree that you can feel that way. But the way you were phrasing it made it sound like lighthearted articles like this one don't deserve to be on the wiki, which is bogus.

(not an moderator or any form of staff, in case i was sounding a bit authoritative)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi! Thanks for taking the time to read this, even though you didn't like it. That being said, I'd like to address some of your concerns.

Foundation allowing staff to volunteer

In my opinion, D-Class should only be used when the experiment has a high chance of physical or mental danger. There's not an infinite amount of death row prisoners to draw from. And if you look, they had five tests before they allowed a Foundation Staff member to try it. And they stopped after a man's skin sloughed off and he inverted.

The Childish Demon

He's not childish, he's a college kid mentally. Imagine if you were a mechanic, in your first few months of training, and you got teleported to Hell. As soon as you get there, the demons are like "Hey bud, can you fix this car?" Can you imagine how bad that would go, given your level of inexperience?

Everything else.

I can't see why this feels like a fanfiction, or a webcomic. Sure, there's some comedic parts, but they were put in place to make the vivid imagery for the body horror even more jarring.

Perhaps you should read things that aren't all dark murder evil cold etc. The anomalous doesn't have to be boring, or grimdark. It just has to be not normal, and this? Very abnormal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I come to notice issues with the logic of the story more than enjoy it, I consider it bad. The whole atmosphere of the piece is like that for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The whole thing feels like a fanfiction/webcomic from some far off corner of the internet. I don't know why this got so many upvotes, but I'm really not into it.

This doesn't fit the critique standards IMO

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very far removed from what the wiki is about.

Idk why you're saying this, writers can write about what they want. Of course you're free to downvote if you think it's not good, but there's not really any restrictions on what writers can write about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: omg amazing… I demand this tale

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels very far removed from what the wiki is about.

The foundation allowing staff to volunteer for subversive type deals with a demon, the demon being childish and inexperienced, everyone in the interviews sounds like insecure teenagers, Elvis jokes, daddy issues…

The whole thing feels like a fanfiction/webcomic from some far off corner of the internet. I don't know why this got so many upvotes, but I'm really not into it.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This got more than a few laughs out of me. I especially love the fact that the demon speaks in Californian colloquialisms, which is super fitting.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 4996, reading an anatomy textbook: "Hmmm. So she needs more bones… I got this."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It may interest you to know that the Elvis Possley pun was a complete accident.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've made a super cute little subversion here: I had skipped straight to the testing logs and as I read through I assumed he it was a generic malevolent "deal with the devil", and even then I was having fun reading the 'catches' you came up with.

The reveal that he genuinely wants to help people and is trying his best to give them what (he thinks) they want is beyond adorable enough to have made me smile.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you had written this whole thing just for the Elvis Possley pun I would be neither surprised or downcast. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love everything about this. SCP-4996 is trying so hard, okay?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: good catch! <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just love this. Nitpick: Some inconsistency with the spelling of Dr Waleck/Walech.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its even better if you imagine hes voiced by Robin Williams

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Note taken, thanks for the read! (I changed it to depicted because otherwise, I'd have to rework the sentence structure)

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: ahhhh thank you so much <3!

I enjoyed reading this entry, my only suggestion would be refining the Special Containment Procedures.

The sentence "with the door engraved with the glyphs outlined in Technical Document 4996-2" is a bit awkward and can be improved by changing the wording such as, "the door must be engraved with glyphs depicted in Technical Document 4996-2."

I love 4996,, they're so excited to see the human world, it's so pure,, I especially love how they're like, so eager to help and is so friendly to literally everyone. They're the embodiment of Chaotic Good which is such a refreshing take on a demonic dealmaker character. Easy +1, expect some fanart in the near future☆

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This relates primarily to folklore regarding how demons and fae have an aversion to iron (and most pure metals) and running/holy water.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Id like a little more detail on the part in the containment procedures; What kind of river water does the iron have to be drenched in? And will SCP-4996 breach if the wrong kind of river water drenches the iron? Pardon me if I have not read through the whole thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A Buddy movie would be fantastic but may I suggest the following?

Marvel Stage Six
<3

SCP-4996: Ah! You're an Otherworlder, right? My school covered you in history. I have to know, what can you do?

SCP-2662: Oh wow, so you're uh… a demon? Not what I was expecting.

SCP-4496: You know, people keep saying that and I like, don't get it. But you gotta tell me all the cool stuff you can do. I'm trying to gather superheroes to save the world.

SCP-2662: Um okay… Well, I'm still young, but people form cults around me?

SCP-4996: So they listen to you?

SCP-2662: Not reall-

SCP-4996: I'll give them all superpowers and then!

[SCP-4996 covers its mouth with its hands.]

SCP-4996: We'll have our own Justice League!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm imagining a buddy movie with 4996 and 2662. They can work through their daddy issues together.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the read! I was debating going into the details, but I thought it would detract from the narrative. In the end, it's just a simple summoning circle with a cliche blood ritual. But don't worry, I will most likely return to this "setting" in the future.

I loved the concept of this SCP and i loved how you described it and i loved the story that you made with it i wish you went into a little bit more detail when describing how it was summoned and what it is.

But other than that i love this SCP +1

item.title: Another Lucid Dream!
item.content:encoded:
The road to Hell is paved with good intentions.

Alright, I've got two demon skips under my belt, both of them surreal to write.

Critters:
RockTeethMothEyes and JanuaryWhite, who encouraged me to max out the weirdness and body horror.
Calibri Bold
DrAkimoto
And others! (Make urself known, my memory sucks)
